_sources.discrepancies: NCSL classifies Nevada as no document required — Nevada verifies voter identity through signature comparison on mail ballots and does not require ID to vote in person (voter states name and address). Nevada is not a universal vote-by-mail state but all active registered voters receive a mail ballot automatically for primary and general elections under AB 321 (2021). 2026 Nevada primary estimated June 9 (second Tuesday in June). TODO verify exact 2026 cycle dates with Nevada SoS.

earlyVoting.notes: All active registered voters receive a mail ballot automatically. In-person early voting available at polling locations statewide. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.

All active registered voters receive a mail ballot automatically approximately 2 weeks before the election. In-person early voting locations available statewide. Same-day registration available through Election Day.

Nevada automatically sends mail ballots to all active registered voters. In-person early voting is also available at multiple locations in each county for approximately 14 days before Election Day. Same-day voter registration is available through Election Day at in-person voting locations.

acceptedIds: No ID document required — voters state their name and address at the polls
Signature comparison on mail ballots is primary verification mechanism
If poll worker cannot verify identity, voter may cast a provisional ballot
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Nevada law prohibits photographing your marked ballot for the purpose of showing how you voted. Nevada is a no-ID-required state; poll workers verify identity by matching voter records. Contact your county for local polling place rules.
additionalRules: Nevada has a closed primary — only voters registered with a political party may vote in that party's primary. Non-partisan and minor party voters receive a separate ballot.
Same-day voter registration is available at in-person voting locations through Election Day.
All active registered voters automatically receive a mail ballot before each election (AB 321, 2021).
No ID required in person — simply state your name and address.

eligibility: All active registered voters automatically receive a ballot by mail before each primary and general election
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: No application required — ballots are automatically mailed to all active registered voters
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by 7:00 p.m. on Election Day, or postmarked by Election Day and received within 7 days
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Return by 7 p.m. on Election Day in person or at a drop box, or mail postmarked by Election Day (received within 7 days).

primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEn: Nevada has a closed primary (Nev. Rev. Stat. §293.175). Only voters registered with a political party may vote in that party's primary. Voters registered as nonpartisan or with a minor party that is not holding a primary may not vote in the major party primaries.
primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEs: Nevada tiene una primaria cerrada (Nev. Rev. Stat. §293.175). Solo los votantes registrados en un partido político pueden votar en la primaria de ese partido. Los votantes registrados como no partidistas o con un partido menor que no celebra primaria no pueden votar en las primarias de los partidos principales.
